notes: The note is structurally complete, adopts a corrected calibrated exponent, proves a conditional feasible upper bound with a matching polynomial lower converse, and positions the contribution at field level.

structure.notes: All required Stage 0 sections, locked Stat parameters, labelled assumptions, theorem statements, proof roles, sanity checks, minimality checks, and an actionable Stat-oriented formalization checklist are present.

novelty.notes: The theorem-level contribution is plausibly field-level: a calibrated margin-overlap policy-regret exponent with a clipped-AIPW achievability theorem and two-point converse, positioned against policy learning, margin-rate, weak-overlap, and VC-process literature.

correctness.notes: Adopting D0.5.1: the clipped-AIPW drift, finite-VC fixed point, lower-bound overlap calibration, chi-square scale, regret separation, and known-logging nuisance compatibility were reproduced with no blocking defect.

journal_recommendations.fit_reasoning: The result targets observational policy learning under weak overlap and gives a theorem-level rate/converse contribution of direct interest to econometric treatment-choice and causal-inference readers.
The paper has a clear econometric decision-theory angle, with policy regret, offline observational data, and a sharp polynomial exponent rather than an algorithm-benchmark contribution.
The margin-overlap minimax rate and clipped-score empirical-process analysis fit methodological statistics audiences working on causal learning and statistical decision rules.
